meta.description: Iron Collective faction questline — 100 nodes covering rise through a workers' revolutionary union

ic_001.title: Foundry District Gate
text: The Foundry District smells like burning metal and exhaustion. Smokestacks rise like broken fingers against the amber sky, venting chemical haze that hasn't cleared since the first factories were built three generations ago. Workers stream through the main gate in grey coveralls, heads bowed, shoulders hunched against the grit that falls from overhead conveyor lines.

You've heard rumors about a workers' movement organizing in this district. Something called the Iron Collective. The kind of whisper that gets people disappeared by corporate security if it reaches the wrong ears. But the contact who sent you here said to look for a red gear symbol painted on the wall near Blast Furnace Seven.
The gate guard, a bored-looking cyborg with a scanner for a left eye, waves workers through without checking IDs. The shift change rush is your best cover.

choices.text: Blend in with the workers and slip through the gate
Try to fast-talk the gate guard for information

ic_002.title: Among the Workers
text: You fall into step with the shift workers, matching their pace, their posture, the way they keep their eyes fixed on the ground two meters ahead. Nobody questions you. In the Foundry District, one body looks like every other body when it's covered in soot and resignation.

The red gear symbol appears on the wall near Blast Furnace Seven, just as promised. It's small, painted in what looks like rust-colored primer, easy to mistake for an actual rust stain if you aren't looking for it. Below it, someone has scratched three words into the metal: THE IRON HOLDS.
A woman in a welder's mask leans against the wall near the symbol. She's pretending to adjust her glove, but her eyes behind the tinted visor are tracking everyone who passes.

choices.text: Approach the woman and mention you're looking for the Collective
Linger nearby and wait for her to make the first move

ic_003.title: A Useful Conversation
text: The gate guard's scanner-eye rotates toward you, but your easy confidence disarms him. You spin a story about being a new transfer from the east-side smelting plant, and he buys it. More than buys it — he leans in conspiratorially.

'You're one of the new organizers, yeah?' he mutters, his voice barely audible over the machinery. 'Look, I didn't see you. But the woman by Furnace Seven, the one with the welder's mask? She's who you want. Tell her Riggs sent you. She'll know what it means.'
He waves you through with a knowing nod. Apparently the Collective has sympathizers even among the gate staff.

choices.text: Head to Blast Furnace Seven and find the woman

ic_004.title: Cold Shoulder
text: The gate guard's scanner-eye focuses on you with sudden intensity. 'Move along,' he says flatly. 'No loitering. You're either on shift or you're trespassing.' His hand drifts toward the alarm panel on his belt.
You back off and join the flow of workers instead, slipping through the gate with the crowd. Not every door opens with charm. Some you just have to walk through.

choices.text: Find Blast Furnace Seven on your own

ic_005.title: Spark
text: The woman lifts her welder's mask as you approach, revealing a face mapped with old burn scars and bright, intelligent eyes. One of her arms is a custom-built prosthetic, clearly self-modified — wires trail from the forearm panel and the fingers move with a precision that factory-standard chrome never achieves.

'You're either brave, stupid, or a corporate plant,' she says. 'I'm Spark. And I've got about ninety seconds before the shift supervisor's drone does another sweep. So talk fast. Why are you looking for us?'

text.text: 'Wait,' she interrupts before you can answer. 'Riggs sent you? That changes things. He doesn't vouch for people lightly. Alright, you've got my attention.'

choices.text: "I want to help. The workers here deserve better than this."
"I've got skills you can use. I'm looking for work that matters."
"I heard the Iron Collective is the only group standing up to the corporations."

ic_006.title: Observed
text: You lean against a support column thirty meters away, pretending to check a datapad. For ten minutes, nothing happens. The woman adjusts her gloves. Workers pass. Machinery groans. Then, without looking directly at you, she speaks — and somehow you hear her voice through the datapad's speaker.

'I hacked your pad's microphone about eight minutes ago,' the voice says. 'You've been standing there long enough to make me nervous. I'm Spark. If you're corporate, you should know I've already mapped three exit routes and your datapad is now broadcasting your location to some very unfriendly people. If you're not corporate, walk over here and let's talk like adults.'

choices.text: Walk over and introduce yourself properly

ic_007.title: The Invitation
text: Spark studies you for a long moment. Whatever she's looking for, she seems to find it — or at least enough of it to take a chance.

'Pretty words,' she says. 'But the Collective doesn't recruit based on speeches. We recruit based on action. There's a group of workers in the Stamping Mill who haven't been paid in three cycles. Corporate says it's a processing error. We say it's punishment for filing a safety complaint after two people died on the line last month.'

'The workers are scared. They won't organize on their own. But if someone went in there, talked to them, showed them they're not alone?' She taps the red gear symbol on the wall. 'That's how the Iron Collective grows. One conversation at a time.'
'You do this, you come back to me, and I'll take you to meet the Forge Master himself. Deal?'

choices.text: "Deal. Where's the Stamping Mill?"
"What's the catch? This sounds too straightforward."

ic_008.title: Spark's Warning
text: Spark's expression hardens. 'The catch is that corporate security has flagged the Stamping Mill as a potential unrest zone. They've posted undercover monitors in the workforce. You say the wrong thing to the wrong person, and you vanish into a corporate detention facility that officially doesn't exist.'
'The catch is that two workers who tried to organize last month were found dead in a coolant tank. Official report said accident. The bruises on their necks said otherwise.'
'The catch,' she finishes quietly, 'is that everything we do carries a price, and the people who pay it are never the ones who can afford to. So. You still in?'

choices.text: "I'm in. Point me to the Stamping Mill."
"I need to think about this." (Leave)

ic_009.title: The Stamping Mill
text: The Stamping Mill is a cathedral of noise. Hydraulic presses slam down every four seconds with enough force to flatten a transport. The floor vibrates so hard your teeth ache. Workers in blast-shielding gear feed sheets of industrial alloy into the machines, their movements mechanical and precise, because a moment's distraction means losing a hand.

You find the unpaid workers gathered near the back during a water break. There are about twenty of them. Their faces are hollow with the specific kind of anger that comes from being cheated by people who know you can't fight back. A tall woman with grease-stained hands seems to be their informal leader.

'Another cycle, another empty account,' she's saying to the group. 'Kenji's daughter needs her meds. Okoye can't make rent. And what does the company say? Processing error. Three goddamn cycles of processing error.'

choices.text: Step forward and address the group directly
Talk to the tall woman privately first
Look around for corporate monitors before doing anything

ic_010.title: The Speech
text: You step into the circle of workers and every eye turns to you. Strangers don't just walk up to a group of angry, unpaid laborers in the Foundry District. Not unless they're selling something or setting a trap.

But you speak from a place they recognize. You talk about what it means to give your body to a machine for ten hours a day and have the people who profit from your suffering pretend they can't find the credits to pay you. You talk about the two workers who died and the report that called it an accident. You watch their anger harden into something with edges.
'There are people who will stand with you,' you tell them. 'You don't have to fight alone. The Iron Collective doesn't make promises it can't keep. But it keeps every promise it makes.'
The tall woman stares at you for a long time. Then she nods, slowly. 'What do you need from us?'

choices.text: "Organize. Choose representatives. Document everything."
"Stop working. A strike is the only language corporate understands."

ic_011.title: A Cold Reception
text: You step forward, but the words come out wrong. Too polished, too rehearsed. You sound like a corporate motivational broadcast, and these people have heard enough of those to last a lifetime.

'Who the hell are you?' the tall woman asks, stepping between you and the group. 'You don't work here. You don't smell like this place. You show up during our break and start making speeches? Get out before I call the floor supervisor.'
The other workers close ranks behind her. You've lost the room before you ever had it.

choices.text: Try a different approach — talk to the woman privately
Leave and report back to Spark

ic_012.title: Voss
text: You approach the tall woman when she separates from the group to refill her water canister. Up close, you can see the burns on her forearms, the kind that come from years of working too close to superheated metal. Her name tag reads VOSS.

'I know what you are,' Voss says before you can open your mouth. 'I've heard the rumors about the Collective. I've seen the gear symbols on the walls.' She glances around, then drops her voice. 'Half my people are ready to snap. The other half are too scared to breathe wrong. If you're offering help, I'm listening. But if this is some corporate loyalty test, I swear I'll throw you into a press myself.'
You explain what Spark told you. Voss listens without expression. When you finish, she stares at the water running from the canister.

choices.text: "The Collective can protect your people. But they need to stand together."
"I won't lie to you. This is dangerous. But doing nothing is worse."

ic_013.title: Spotting the Monitor
text: You hang back, scanning the mill floor with careful eyes. There. A man near the eastern press line who isn't keeping pace with the machines. His coveralls are too clean. His gloves show no wear. And every thirty seconds, his hand drifts to his right ear in a gesture that would look like scratching an itch if you weren't looking for it. He's wearing a concealed comm unit.
Corporate monitor. Spark warned you about these. If you approach the workers while he's watching, anything you say will be recorded and relayed to security within the hour.
You also notice a blind spot in the drone surveillance pattern near the south exit, where two broken cameras create a gap in coverage. That could be useful.

choices.text: Create a distraction to draw the monitor away
Wait for the monitor's shift to end, then approach the workers

ic_014.title: Seeds Planted
text: By the time the break horn sounds, you've spoken with nine workers individually. Four are committed. Three are considering it. Two said no but promised they wouldn't talk. In the Foundry District, silence is its own form of solidarity.

Voss walks you to the south exit. 'I'll organize the ones who are ready,' she says. 'We'll start documenting. Shift logs, pay discrepancies, the safety violations they covered up after Kenji and Okoye's friends died. When you come back with your Collective people, we'll have something to show them.'
She grips your arm with a hand strong enough to bend rebar. 'Don't make me regret this.'
You leave the Stamping Mill with a list of names encrypted on your datapad and the weight of twenty people's hopes sitting in your chest.

choices.text: Return to Spark with the good news

ic_015.title: Strike Talk
text: The word strike moves through the group like an electric current. Some faces light up. Others go pale. Voss grabs your arm and pulls you aside.

'Are you insane?' she hisses. 'A strike right now, without organization, without support? They'll fire everyone and replace us with temps by tomorrow. Half these people have families. You can't just throw that word around like it doesn't have consequences.'
She's right. A premature strike would be a disaster. But the anger in the room is real, and anger has a shelf life.

choices.text: "You're right. We organize first. Build a foundation."
"The anger is the foundation. We strike while it's hot."

ic_016.title: Reporting Failure
text: You find Spark where you left her, by Blast Furnace Seven. She reads your face before you say a word.

'Didn't go well?' She sighs. 'It happens. These people have been beaten down so long they flinch when someone offers a hand. Doesn't mean you stop offering.' She pushes off the wall. 'Come on. I'll take you in through the back. We'll try a different approach. Sometimes the Forge Master himself needs to make the pitch.'
She starts walking toward a cargo tunnel marked AUTHORIZED PERSONNEL ONLY, and despite the sign, she walks like she owns the place.

choices.text: Follow Spark into the tunnel

ic_017.title: The Distraction
text: You trigger a cascade failure in one of the hydraulic regulators near the eastern press line. Nothing dangerous — just enough to send pressurized coolant spraying across the floor in a dramatic hiss of steam. The corporate monitor reflexively moves toward the commotion, hand pressed to his ear, reporting the incident.
While he's distracted, you have a clear window. You move to Voss and the workers, speaking quickly and quietly in the fog of coolant vapor. The monitor won't be gone long.
The conversation is compressed and urgent, but sometimes urgency is what cuts through fear.

choices.text: Make your pitch to the workers before the monitor returns

ic_018.title: Spark's Approval
text: Spark reviews the encrypted list on your datapad, scrolling through names and notes with the speed of someone who's been doing this for years. A rare smile crosses her scarred face.
'Nine contacts. Four committed. And you didn't get caught by the monitor.' She looks up. 'Not bad for your first run. The Forge Master will want to hear about this.'

She pulls a lever disguised as a pipe valve, and a section of wall slides back to reveal a descending staircase. Warm air rises from below, carrying the sound of hammering and the smell of honest sweat.
'Welcome to the Underhammer,' Spark says. 'Try not to stare.'

choices.text: Descend into the Underhammer

ic_019.title: Premature Strike
text: Against Voss's protests, you push for immediate action. Eight workers put down their tools. The stamping presses grind to a halt one by one. For exactly eleven minutes, the Stamping Mill is silent for the first time in decades.

Then corporate security arrives. Not the bored guards from the gate — Meridian Sec-Corp tactical units in full riot gear. They don't negotiate. They don't warn. They deploy crowd-dispersal charges that lock every muscle in your body with electromagnetic pulses.
When you wake up in a holding cell, Spark is there, having paid your bail with Collective funds. Her expression is thunderous.

'Three workers hospitalized. Five fired. Voss is in corporate detention and we can't reach her.' She stares at you. 'You wanted to change the world in one afternoon. The world pushed back. Learn from it or leave.'

choices.text: "I made a mistake. I'm sorry. What can I do to fix this?"
"The strike proved the workers are ready. We just need better planning."

ic_020.title: The Underhammer
text: The Underhammer is built in the bones of an abandoned smelting facility three levels below the Foundry District. Someone has converted the old crucible chambers into living quarters, the cooling tunnels into corridors, and the main casting floor into a meeting hall large enough to hold two hundred people.

Everywhere you look, the red gear symbol is painted, welded, or stamped into surfaces. Workers move with purpose — maintaining equipment, studying tactical maps projected onto sheet metal walls, running training drills in a cleared area near the south wall. This isn't a protest group. This is an underground army.

At the center of the hall, standing before an anvil that has been converted into a command table, is a man who looks like he was forged rather than born. Seven feet tall, arms like industrial pistons, skin darkened by decades of foundry work. His left hand is a massive mechanical prosthetic that glows faintly at the joints with internal heat.
Forge Master Dran.

choices.text: Approach Forge Master Dran
Look around the Underhammer before meeting the leader

ic_021.title: The Forge Master
text: Dran doesn't look up as you approach. He's studying a holographic map of the Foundry District, his mechanical hand tracing supply routes and marking security patrol patterns with gestures that leave faint trails of heat in the air.

'Spark says you have potential,' he says. His voice is like grinding stone — deep, slow, and worn smooth by use. 'She also says you have a tendency to act before you think. In this work, that tendency kills people. Not you. The people who trusted you.'

He finally looks at you. His eyes are startlingly gentle in that brutal face. 'I was a press operator for thirty-one years. I watched six friends die on the line. Each time, the company said it was an accident. Each time, they refused to fix the machines that killed them. I started the Iron Collective because I got tired of attending funerals for people who died making rich men richer.'
'So here is my question for you: Are you here because you want to feel like a hero, or are you here because you can't sleep knowing what's happening to these people?'

choices.text: "I can't sleep. I've seen what this system does to people and I can't look away anymore."
"I'm here because I have skills that can make a difference."
"Both. Does it matter, as long as the work gets done?"

ic_022.title: The Underhammer Tour
text: You wander through the Underhammer's corridors. In one chamber, a medic treats a worker's chemical burns while explaining labor safety regulations that the companies ignore. In another, someone teaches a group of teenagers how to encrypt communications on salvaged datapads.

A workshop near the armory produces equipment from salvaged materials — signal jammers, EMP grenades, reinforced body armor made from repurposed industrial plating. Nothing fancy, but all of it functional.

On a wall near the mess hall, someone has painted a mural. It shows workers rising from beneath a factory, breaking through the floor, their raised fists transforming into gears that turn the machinery of a new world. Below it, in careful lettering: WE ARE THE MACHINE. WE DECIDE WHAT IT BUILDS.
A large man with a shaved head and hands like hammers watches you from a doorway. His arms are crossed, his expression unfriendly. A name tag on his vest reads HAMMER.

choices.text: Approach Hammer and introduce yourself
Go meet Forge Master Dran

ic_023.title: Accepted
text: Dran nods slowly, and something shifts in his expression. Not trust — not yet — but recognition. He sees something in you that he's seen before, probably in the mirror.

'Then welcome to the Iron Collective,' he says. He extends his mechanical hand. The heat from it is palpable, like shaking hands with a furnace. 'You start as a Laborer. Everyone does. No rank is given that isn't earned. You'll work alongside the people you want to save, and you'll learn what they endure every day.'

He assigns you a bunk in the worker quarters and a shift in the Collective's underground workshop. 'Spark will be your handler. Hammer will be your reality check. Between the two of them, you'll either become something useful or you'll wash out. Either way, you'll have earned whatever you get.'
The Iron Collective doesn't applaud new members. Dran goes back to his maps. Workers go back to their tasks. You're part of the machine now, and the machine doesn't stop.

choices.text: Begin your work as a Laborer

ic_024.title: Conditional Acceptance
text: Dran's expression doesn't change. 'Skills are tools,' he says. 'Tools don't have loyalty. People do.' He lets the silence stretch until it becomes uncomfortable.

'I'll give you a chance. Not because I trust you, but because we need hands and we can't afford to turn them away. You'll start at the bottom like everyone else. Prove your loyalty with actions, not words, and you'll rise. Betray us, and Hammer will make sure you regret it for whatever's left of your life.'
He waves you toward the worker quarters. The dismissal is absolute.

choices.text: Begin your work as a Laborer

ic_025.title: Hammer
text: Hammer unfolds his arms as you approach. Each forearm is thicker than your thigh. He's not augmented — this is raw, earned muscle from decades of manual labor. A network of scars covers his knuckles like a topographic map of every fight he's ever been in.

'So you're the new recruit,' he says. His voice is surprisingly quiet for someone his size. 'Let me be clear about something. I've been with Dran since the beginning. I was there when we had nothing but anger and six people in a basement. I've bled for this cause. I've killed for it, when I had to.'
He leans down, bringing his face level with yours. 'If you bring trouble to our door, I won't wait for an explanation. Understood?'
Behind the threat, you sense something else. Not cruelty. Protectiveness. Hammer loves these people, and that love has made him dangerous.

choices.text: "Understood. I'll earn your trust."
"You don't scare me. But I respect what you've built here."

ic_026.title: The Laborer's Life
text: Your first weeks in the Iron Collective are a blur of exhaustion and education. You work the underground workshop by day, manufacturing supplies and maintaining equipment. By night, you attend strategy sessions where Spark teaches operational security and Hammer runs combat drills.
You learn the Collective's structure: a cell system where no single member knows more than five others by name. If one person is captured, the damage is contained. It's paranoid and necessary.

Spark approaches you one morning with two assignments. 'Pick one,' she says. 'The Collective needs a supply run into Neon Court territory to source medical equipment. Or we need someone to investigate reports of a new corporate surveillance system being installed in the eastern factories. Both are dangerous. Both matter.'

choices.text: "I'll run the supply mission into Neon Court territory."
"I'll investigate the surveillance system."

ic_027.title: Neon Court Territory
text: The border between the Foundry District and Neon Court territory is marked by a wall of light. On this side: grime, smoke, and the sound of machinery. On that side: holographic advertisements sixty stories tall, streets paved with luminescent panels, and people dressed in fabrics that cost more than a factory worker makes in a year.

Your contact is a Neon Court medic named Lira who secretly sympathizes with the Collective's cause. You're supposed to meet her at a club called The Prism. The cover story is that you're a maintenance technician servicing the club's lighting system.
Two Neon Court enforcers patrol the entrance. They're sleek, augmented, and radiating the casual arrogance of people who've never been hungry.

choices.text: Use the maintenance cover story to get past the enforcers
Find a back entrance to avoid the enforcers entirely

ic_028.title: The Prism
text: Inside The Prism, light is currency. Every surface refracts, reflects, or emits luminescence. The clientele are Neon Court elite — augmented beauties with chrome skin and holographic hair, executives in suits that shimmer with embedded display panels, and the occasional celebrity whose face you recognize from the district-wide broadcasts.

Lira finds you at the service bar. She's young, nervous, and wearing medical scrubs under a designer jacket that doesn't fit. She slides a case across the counter — inside, you find surgical-grade nanite injectors, anti-radiation meds, and a portable tissue regenerator. Equipment worth a fortune on the black market.

'This is everything I could get without triggering an inventory audit,' she whispers. 'The Collective saved my brother's life when the corporations cut his healthcare after his injury. This is me paying that debt.'

choices.text: Thank Lira and leave quickly with the supplies
Ask Lira if she can be a long-term contact inside the Neon Court

ic_029.title: Caught at the Door
text: The enforcers see through your act. One of them grabs your arm while the other scans your ID chip. 'Foundry District registration,' the scanner says with audible contempt. 'What's a grinder doing in Neon Court?'

Before it escalates, a woman steps out of the club. 'That's my maintenance tech,' she says, flashing a staff credential. 'There a problem?' The enforcers release you with a warning glare. Inside, the woman introduces herself as Lira.
'You almost blew my cover,' she mutters, handing you a medical supply case. 'Take this and get out. And next time, send someone who can lie better.'

choices.text: Apologize and leave with the supplies

ic_030.title: The Surveillance System
text: The eastern factory complex is a maze of processing plants and assembly lines. Spark has identified three locations where new hardware has been installed — unmarked black boxes hardwired into the factory network. They could be anything from productivity monitors to something far more invasive.

You reach the first installation point. The black box is mounted high on a support column, connected to the factory's power grid and data network by armored cables. A small red light blinks on its surface.
'Get close enough to plug in my scanner,' Spark says through your earpiece. 'I need sixty seconds of direct connection to pull the firmware.'

choices.text: Climb the support column and connect the scanner
Try to hack the connection remotely from a terminal

ic_031.title: Data Retrieved
text: You connect the scanner and hold your breath for sixty agonizing seconds. Spark's voice is a constant murmur in your ear, narrating the data stream. When the scan completes, there's a long silence.

'This is bad,' Spark finally says. 'These aren't productivity monitors. They're neural pattern scanners. They're reading brainwave signatures of every worker who passes within ten meters. Cross-referencing them against a database of known dissident patterns. Corporate isn't just watching for trouble — they're building a tool to predict it.'

'If they complete this installation across all the eastern factories, they'll be able to identify Collective sympathizers before those people even know they're sympathizers. We need to bring this to Dran. Now.'

choices.text: Rush back to the Underhammer with the data

ic_032.title: Alarm Triggered
text: Your attempt triggers a security alert. Red lights strobe across the factory floor and a siren begins its ascending wail. Workers scatter from their stations in practiced panic — they've seen this before.

You manage to complete the scan just as security drones converge on your position. Spark guides you through a ventilation shaft that leads to an abandoned utility corridor. You crawl through spaces not designed for human bodies, scraping skin on rusted metal, until you emerge three blocks away in a drainage culvert.
'Got the data,' Spark reports. 'And it's worse than we thought. These are neural pattern scanners. I'll brief you when you're back. If you're not bleeding too badly.'

choices.text: Head back to the Underhammer

ic_033.title: Mission Debrief
text: Back in the Underhammer, you report your findings to Dran, Spark, and Hammer. Dran listens in silence, his mechanical hand slowly opening and closing — a habit, Spark tells you later, that means he's thinking at a level that usually precedes large-scale action.

'You've done well,' Dran says when you finish. It's the closest thing to praise you've heard from him. 'Both missions yielded results. The medical supplies will save lives. And the surveillance data...' He pauses. 'The surveillance data may save the entire movement.'

He turns to address a gathering of senior members. 'We're accelerating our timeline. I want a full strategic assessment of every factory in the eastern complex by week's end. And I want our new Laborer promoted to Organizer. They've earned it.'
Hammer grunts — whether in approval or displeasure is impossible to tell.

choices.text: Accept the promotion and prepare for the next phase

ic_034.title: Lira's Decision
text: Lira's eyes widen. 'A long-term contact? I...' She looks around the club, at the wealth and comfort surrounding her. Then she thinks about her brother, about the workers who saved him.

'Yes,' she says, her voice steadying. 'I have access to Neon Court medical records, supply chain data, even some security protocols. If the Collective can protect my identity, I can feed you intelligence regularly.'
'But you need to understand — if they catch me, they won't just fire me. The Neon Court doesn't create martyrs. They create examples. I'm trusting you with my life.'
You give her an encrypted comm channel and a dead-drop protocol that Spark designed. When you leave The Prism, you've gained more than medical supplies. You've gained a spy.

choices.text: Return to the Underhammer with the supplies and the news

ic_035.title: The Organizer
text: As an Organizer, your world expands. You're given access to the Collective's cell network — not all of it, but enough to coordinate operations across three factory districts. You attend strategy meetings with Dran's inner circle. You learn the names of allies you never knew existed and enemies who are closer than you feared.

Weeks pass. You organize twelve factory cells, coordinate three successful supply runs, and help evacuate a family whose father was identified as a Collective sympathizer before corporate security could act.
Then Spark pulls you aside with news that changes everything.

'We've intercepted a corporate communication,' she says. 'Meridian Industries is planning to close the Stamping Mill. Not because it's unprofitable — because the workers there filed that safety complaint. They're going to shut the whole operation down and relocate to an automated facility in Sector 12. Five hundred workers will lose everything.'

choices.text: "We have to warn Voss and the Stamping Mill workers."
"Can we use this information as leverage? Force Meridian to negotiate?"
"What if we sabotage the Sector 12 facility before it's operational?"

ic_036.title: Warning Voss
text: You find Voss on the mill floor, supervising a shift change. She's thinner than when you last saw her, the kind of weight loss that comes from stress rather than hunger. When you tell her about Meridian's plan, she doesn't react visibly. Years of factory work have taught her to absorb bad news without flinching.
'Five hundred families,' she says quietly. 'Mine included.' She watches her workers, the people she's fought for. 'How long do we have?'
'Three weeks,' you tell her. 'Maybe four.'
'Then we don't have time to be subtle. Call a meeting. Every worker who's willing to listen. We'll need a plan that makes shutting us down more expensive than keeping us open.'

choices.text: Help Voss organize a mass meeting of Stamping Mill workers

ic_037.title: The Leverage Play
text: Spark considers your proposal. 'Leverage requires leverage,' she says. 'We'd need something Meridian can't afford to have public. Their safety record alone would embarrass them, but embarrassment doesn't stop corporations. Money does.'

She pulls up financial records on her terminal. 'If we can prove that Meridian's closure is retaliation for the safety complaint — which it is — that's a labor law violation even by this city's corrupt standards. The fines alone would cost them more than running the Stamping Mill for another decade.'

'But we'd need evidence. Hard evidence. Internal memos, board meeting recordings, executive communications. The kind of data that lives behind corporate firewalls that would fry my brain if I tried to crack them alone.'
'Unless,' she adds slowly, 'we had someone on the inside.'

choices.text: "I'll infiltrate Meridian's corporate offices."
"What about Lira? She has Neon Court connections."
"That's too risky. Let's warn the workers instead."

ic_038.title: The Sabotage Option
text: Hammer steps forward. 'Now you're speaking my language,' he says with the first smile you've seen from him. It's not a comforting smile.

But Dran raises his mechanical hand. 'Sabotage is a tool, not a strategy. If we destroy the Sector 12 facility, Meridian builds another one. If we destroy that, they build another. We don't have the resources for an endless war of attrition against a corporation with more money than most governments.'

'However,' he continues, 'if sabotage is combined with other pressure — legal, public, economic — it becomes a bargaining chip. A demonstration that the cost of fighting us is higher than the cost of listening to us.'

He looks at you. 'I'll authorize a measured operation against the Sector 12 facility. But you will also warn the Stamping Mill workers and prepare them for a legal fight. We attack on every front or we don't attack at all.'

choices.text: "Understood. Multi-front approach."

ic_039.title: The Mass Meeting
text: Three hundred and twelve workers crowd into the Stamping Mill's cargo bay after the night shift ends. The air is thick with tension, body heat, and the sharp smell of machine oil. Voss stands on a shipping crate at the front. You stand beside her.

She tells them the truth. No softening, no spin. Meridian is closing the mill. Five hundred jobs gone. No severance. No relocation assistance. Because they had the audacity to demand safe working conditions.

The silence after she finishes is worse than shouting. Then a man in the back — old, stooped, hands shaking with either palsy or rage — stands up. 'I gave this company forty-three years,' he says. 'And they want to throw me away like a broken tool. I say we fight. I don't care how.'
The dam breaks. Voices erupt. Arguments, plans, fears, fury. In the chaos, Voss looks at you.

choices.text: Advocate for a structured, legal resistance campaign
Call for a full strike — shut the Stamping Mill down on their own terms
Propose a combined approach: public pressure and covert sabotage

ic_040.title: Corporate Infiltration
text: Spark provides a forged identity — a mid-level data analyst transferring from Meridian's off-world division. The credentials are perfect, she assures you. The cover story will hold for seventy-two hours before the real transfer records are cross-checked.

Meridian's corporate tower is everything the Foundry District isn't. Clean air, climate control, floors that don't vibrate. The executives move through corridors of polished chrome with the unhurried confidence of people who have never worried about their next meal.
You reach the data analytics floor. The executive communications server is three levels up, behind biometric locks and an AI security system.
You have two days to find the evidence before your cover evaporates.

choices.text: Hack the executive server directly
Social engineer an executive assistant for access

ic_041.title: Lira's Intel
text: You contact Lira through the encrypted channel. She's terrified but willing. Within forty-eight hours, she delivers a data package that Spark calls 'a smoking gun wrapped in a confession.'

Internal memos from Meridian's board of directors explicitly ordering the Stamping Mill closure as 'corrective action' against 'labor insubordination.' Financial projections showing the mill is profitable. And most damning: an executive's personal log discussing how the closure will serve as 'a warning to other facilities that might consider similar organized resistance.'

'This is enough to trigger a full labor investigation,' Spark says, her eyes bright. 'If we can get it to the right people. And by the right people, I don't mean the bought-and-paid-for Labor Commission. I mean the public.'

choices.text: "Let's leak it to every news outlet in the city."
"Use it as blackmail. Force Meridian to keep the mill open."

ic_042.title: The Legal Path
text: You outline a structured campaign: document every safety violation, every unpaid wage, every instance of corporate retaliation. File formal complaints with the Labor Commission. Build a case so airtight that even the city's corrupt legal system can't ignore it.
Some workers nod. Others shake their heads. 'The legal system is owned by the same people who own the mills,' one woman says. 'We'll spend years in courts while our families starve.'

She's not wrong. But the legal path has one advantage: legitimacy. If the Collective is seen as a lawful workers' organization rather than a terrorist cell, they gain public sympathy. And public sympathy is a weapon that corporations fear more than any bomb.
Voss calls a vote. The room splits roughly even.

choices.text: Convince the holdouts with a passionate speech
Compromise — legal fight backed by covert action as insurance

ic_043.title: Strike Call
text: The word strike hits different when three hundred people hear it together. It's not a suggestion — it's a detonation. Workers leap to their feet. Fists pound on machinery. Someone starts a chant that rolls through the crowd like thunder: 'THE IRON HOLDS. THE IRON HOLDS.'

Voss grabs your arm. 'If we do this, there's no going back,' she says over the noise. 'Corporate will send security. People will get hurt. Some might not come home.' She searches your face. 'Are you ready for that?'

From the back of the room, Hammer's voice cuts through the chanting. He's arrived with a squad of Collective members, armed and armored. 'Dran sends his support,' he announces. 'The Collective stands with the Stamping Mill. Let's show these corporate bastards what happens when the machine stops.'

choices.text: "We're ready. Shut it all down."
"We strike, but no violence. Peaceful resistance only."

ic_044.title: The Combined Strategy
text: You propose a two-track approach: a public-facing campaign of legal resistance, media outreach, and peaceful protest, backed by a covert team handling sabotage, intelligence gathering, and contingency planning. The fist and the handshake.
Dran would approve. It's exactly his philosophy — pressure from every direction until the wall cracks.
Voss assigns team leaders. The public campaign will be led by workers with clean records and sympathetic stories. The covert operations will be run through Collective cells. You'll coordinate both.
'This is the biggest thing we've attempted,' Voss says after the meeting disperses. 'If it works, it changes everything. If it fails...' She doesn't finish the sentence. She doesn't need to.

choices.text: Begin coordinating the dual campaign

ic_045.title: Evidence Secured
text: Your heart hammers as data streams across your screen. Executive communications, board meeting transcripts, financial analyses that explicitly calculate the 'reputational cost' of worker deaths versus the 'operational cost' of safety improvements. They knew. They always knew. They did the math and decided that dead workers were cheaper than safe machines.

You copy everything to an encrypted drive and walk out of Meridian's corporate tower with enough evidence to bury them. The elevator ride to the ground floor takes forty-seven seconds. It feels like forty-seven years.
Outside, the Foundry District's toxic air has never tasted sweeter.

choices.text: Rush the evidence back to the Underhammer

ic_046.title: Compromised
text: An alarm screams. Red lights strobe across the analytics floor. Your forged credentials just bounced off Meridian's security AI, and now every exit is locking down.
You manage to grab a partial data dump before the terminal locks you out — some executive emails, a fragment of a board meeting transcript. Not the full evidence package you needed, but something.

Spark guides you to an emergency exit through the building's maintenance infrastructure. You drop six stories through a waste disposal chute and emerge in a recycling processing center, covered in filth but alive.
'Partial data,' Spark confirms when she reviews your haul. 'Enough to be useful. Not enough to be definitive. We'll have to supplement this with other sources.'

choices.text: Report back to Dran with what you have

ic_047.title: The Propaganda Campaign
text: Spark works through the night, scrubbing metadata and preparing the evidence for distribution. By morning, the story hits every independent news feed in the city: MERIDIAN INDUSTRIES CLOSING PROFITABLE FACTORY IN RETALIATION AGAINST SAFETY COMPLAINTS. EXECUTIVE MEMOS REVEAL DEAD WORKERS DEEMED 'ACCEPTABLE LOSS.'

The reaction is immediate and volcanic. Workers in factories across the city share the story. Public protests erupt in three districts. Even some mid-level corporate employees leak statements of support, anonymously of course.

Dran watches the news feeds in the Underhammer, his expression unreadable. 'Good,' he says finally. 'Now comes the hard part. Meridian won't take this lying down. They'll hit back, and they'll hit hard. Prepare our people.'
He turns to you. 'You've exceeded every expectation. Effective immediately, you're promoted to Lieutenant. You'll command your own cell and answer directly to me.'

choices.text: "I won't let you down. What's our next move?"

ic_048.title: The Blackmail Option
text: Dran shakes his head. 'Blackmail makes us criminals. It gives them justification to destroy us. The moment we profit from their crimes instead of exposing them, we become what we claim to fight.'
Hammer disagrees. 'Sometimes you fight fire with fire,' he growls. 'Those workers need their jobs now, not after a legal battle that takes years.'
Spark stays quiet, watching you. This is your call. Dran's moral authority or Hammer's pragmatism.
The evidence sits on the encrypted drive in your hand like a weapon with two barrels.

choices.text: Side with Dran — release the evidence publicly
Side with Hammer — use it as leverage to keep the mill open

ic_049.title: The Strike Begins
text: At 0600 hours, the Stamping Mill falls silent. Every press stops. Every conveyor halts. Four hundred and eighty-seven workers walk off the floor in coordinated unison, leaving their stations exactly as they are — mid-process, mid-cycle, mid-production. The cost to Meridian of restarting the interrupted processes will be staggering.

Workers form picket lines at every entrance. Collective members distribute food, water, and medical supplies. Spark has jammed the factory's security drones. Hammer has positioned teams at strategic chokepoints in case corporate sends tactical response.
For the first time in the Stamping Mill's history, the workers are the ones in control.
The news feeds pick up the story within the hour. By midday, workers at two other Meridian facilities stage sympathy walkouts.

choices.text: Hold the line and wait for Meridian's response

ic_050.title: Peaceful Resistance
text: You make the rules clear: no weapons, no destruction, no violence. This is a labor action, not a war. The workers will occupy the factory peacefully and refuse to work until Meridian agrees to negotiate.

Some of Hammer's people look disappointed. Hammer himself studies you with an expression that might be respect or might be pity. 'Peaceful resistance works great,' he says, 'right up until the other side isn't peaceful.'

The occupation proceeds smoothly for the first twelve hours. Workers sing old labor songs. Someone sets up a makeshift kitchen. Children play in the cargo bay while their parents take shifts on the picket line. It almost feels hopeful.
Then the corporate security transports appear on the horizon.

choices.text: Maintain the peaceful stance even as security arrives
Allow Hammer to prepare defensive positions, just in case

ic_051.title: The Mole Hunt
text: Three days after the propaganda campaign launches, disaster strikes. A Collective safe house is raided. Two operatives are arrested. A weapons cache is seized. And worst of all, a list of sympathetic workers is leaked to Meridian — fifty-seven names, all now facing termination or worse.
Someone inside the Iron Collective is feeding information to the corporations.

Dran calls an emergency meeting with only his most trusted people: you, Spark, Hammer, and three senior cell leaders. His face is carved from stone. 'We have a mole,' he says. 'I don't know who. I don't know how long they've been active. But every day they remain in place, more of our people are at risk.'
'I'm giving you this mission personally,' he tells you. 'Find the traitor. Use any means necessary. But be certain before you act. I won't have an innocent person destroyed because we were scared.'

choices.text: Start by analyzing the pattern of leaked information
Interrogate the arrested operatives' known contacts
Set a trap — feed different information to different suspects

ic_052.title: Dangerous Negotiations
text: You arrange a clandestine meeting with a Meridian executive through back channels. The executive, a grey-haired woman named Director Vance, meets you in a neutral location — a decommissioned water treatment plant between districts.
'You have something of ours,' she says without preamble. 'And we have something of yours — five hundred jobs, pensions, and healthcare plans.'

The negotiation is brutal. She offers to keep the mill open in exchange for the evidence and the dismantling of the Collective's cells in the eastern factories. You counter with demands for worker safety improvements and an independent oversight board.

In the end, you reach a compromise. The mill stays open. Safety standards are improved. But the Collective agrees to scale back operations in Meridian territory. Dran is furious. Hammer calls you a sellout. But five hundred families keep their livelihoods.

choices.text: Accept the consequences and continue serving the Collective

ic_053.title: The Corporate Response
text: Meridian's response arrives in the form of twelve armored transports, two crowd-control drones, and Commander Reis — a corporate security officer with a reputation for ending labor disputes with maximum force and minimum paperwork.
Reis broadcasts from the lead transport: 'You are engaged in unlawful occupation of Meridian Industries property. You have ten minutes to disperse. Non-compliance will be met with appropriate force.'
Hammer's teams are ready. The picket lines tighten. Workers who've never held a weapon stand shoulder to shoulder with Collective fighters. Somewhere in the crowd, a child is crying.
Voss finds you. 'Tell me this was worth it,' she says.

choices.text: Try to negotiate with Commander Reis
Hold the line and dare them to attack unarmed workers on camera
Have Spark broadcast the confrontation live to the entire city

ic_054.title: The Line Holds
text: You stand at the front of the picket line. No weapons. No threats. Just bodies in the way of machines, the oldest form of protest there is.
Commander Reis stares at you through the windshield of his transport. His drone operators await orders. The ten-minute timer expires. The crowd-control drones power up their taser arrays.

Then something unexpected happens. The drone operators hesitate. One of them removes her helmet and steps out of the transport. 'My father works in this mill,' she says, her voice cracking. 'I'm not doing this.' She lays her weapon on the ground.
Reis screams orders. But the moment has shifted. Two more operators stand down. Then four. The confrontation dissolves not in violence but in humanity. Corporate security withdraws to regroup.
It's a temporary victory. Reis will come back with loyal troops. But for now, the line holds.

choices.text: Use the victory to rally more support before Reis returns

ic_055.title: Pattern Analysis
text: You spend three days analyzing every piece of information that was leaked, cross-referencing it with who had access to what and when. Spark helps you build a matrix of suspects on her terminal, eliminating names one by one.

The pattern is damning. Every leaked piece of intelligence was accessible to members of Cell Seven — Hammer's personal unit. Specifically, three people had access to all compromised information: a supply coordinator named Fenn, a communications specialist named Rhea, and Hammer's second-in-command, a woman called Torque.
'Torque has been with the Collective since year one,' Spark says, her voice strained. 'If it's her... Hammer will never believe it.'
'And if it's not her and we accuse her,' you reply, 'we destroy one of our best fighters and Hammer's trust forever.'

choices.text: Set a trap for all three suspects simultaneously
Investigate Torque quietly before involving anyone else

ic_056.title: Dead Ends
text: The investigation grinds through interviews, record checks, and surveillance. Everyone is suspicious. Nobody is conclusively guilty. The paranoia spreading through the Collective is almost as damaging as the leaks themselves.

After a week of dead ends, Spark comes to you with a breakthrough. 'I've been monitoring internal communications patterns,' she says. 'Someone has been using a piggyback signal on our encrypted channels — bouncing data to an external receiver during routine message traffic. It's so well hidden that I almost didn't catch it.'
'The signal originates from Cell Seven's communication hub. That narrows it to three people: Fenn, Rhea, or Torque.'

choices.text: Set a trap using the communication channel
Confront all three suspects directly

ic_057.title: The Trap
text: You design a classic canary trap. Three pieces of false intelligence, each unique, each given to a different suspect under the guise of operational briefings. Fenn receives a fabricated supply route. Rhea receives a fake meeting location. Torque receives intelligence about a nonexistent weapons shipment.
Then you wait. Spark monitors every outgoing signal. Hammer is told nothing — you can't risk his reaction tipping off the mole.
Forty-eight hours later, Meridian corporate security dispatches a tactical team to intercept the fake weapons shipment.
The mole is Torque.

choices.text: Tell Dran first and let him decide how to handle it
Confront Torque yourself
Tell Hammer directly — he deserves to hear it from someone who respects him

ic_058.title: Negotiating with Reis
text: Commander Reis agrees to speak with you in a narrow no-man's-land between his transports and the picket line. He's military-precise, chrome-augmented, and deeply uninterested in your cause.

But your words find purchase. You frame the situation in terms he understands: cost-benefit analysis. Attacking workers on camera will generate public backlash that costs Meridian more than the strike itself. A negotiated resolution protects his reputation and Meridian's stock price.

Reis stares at you for a long moment. 'Twelve hours,' he says. 'I'll give you twelve hours to reach a deal with Meridian's labor relations department. After that, I follow my orders.' He turns and walks back to his transport.
Twelve hours. It's not much, but it's everything.

choices.text: Use the twelve hours to negotiate with Meridian

ic_059.title: Reis Won't Listen
text: Commander Reis dismisses you with a wave of his chrome hand. 'I don't negotiate with criminals occupying private property,' he says. 'You've got five minutes to clear these people out, or my drones will do it for you.'
You return to the picket line with nothing. Hammer is already moving his people into defensive positions. The air crackles with impending violence.

Spark grabs your arm. 'I can broadcast this live. Every feed in the city. If Reis attacks unarmed workers on camera, it'll be the biggest PR disaster in Meridian's history. But it means people here will get hurt first.'

choices.text: "Do it. Broadcast everything. The world needs to see this."
"Get the civilians out. We hold the line with Collective fighters only."

ic_060.title: Live Broadcast
text: Spark hijacks every public feed in the city. Millions of screens flicker and display the scene at the Stamping Mill: unarmed workers standing against corporate military hardware. Children behind the picket line. Drones powering up weapons that were designed to control crowds, not protect them.
Commander Reis realizes what's happening. You can see the moment of calculation on his face — the career cost of being the man who ordered an attack broadcast to millions.

He hesitates. In that hesitation, the tide turns. Calls flood into Meridian's corporate headquarters. The stock price drops eleven points in twenty minutes. The city council issues a statement demanding a peaceful resolution.
Reis withdraws his forces. On every screen in the city, workers cheer. The Stamping Mill belongs to the people who built it.

choices.text: Celebrate with the workers, then prepare for what comes next

ic_061.title: Momentum
text: The Stamping Mill victory sends shockwaves through the city. Three more factories join the Collective's network. Workers who were afraid to speak now whisper the name Iron Collective like a prayer. Recruitment triples. Donations pour in from sympathetic citizens across every district.
But success breeds attention, and attention breeds danger. Dran gathers his inner circle.

'The corporations are scared,' he says. 'Scared people do desperate things. We need to move quickly. There's a city council vote next month on the Industrial Workers' Protection Act — legislation that would give workers the right to organize legally. If it passes, we win. If it fails, everything we've built becomes criminal overnight.'
He looks around the room. 'We need to ensure that vote goes our way. And we need to deal with a more immediate problem.' He pauses. 'We have a traitor in our ranks.'

choices.text: "I'll focus on finding the mole."
"Let me work on securing the council vote."

ic_062.title: Investigating Torque
text: You follow Torque for three days. She's disciplined, professional, and seemingly loyal. She runs Hammer's unit with efficiency and genuine care for her people. Nothing in her behavior suggests betrayal.

Then, on the third night, you catch her leaving the Underhammer through a maintenance tunnel that isn't on any map. You follow at a distance, through two kilometers of abandoned infrastructure, until she emerges in a corporate sector. She enters a building with no signage and blacked-out windows.

You wait. Forty minutes later, she leaves. Through a window you glimpse the interior: corporate security monitoring equipment, data terminals, and a holographic display showing the Underhammer's layout.
Torque is the mole. The evidence is irrefutable.

choices.text: Report to Dran immediately
Confront Torque before she can run

ic_063.title: The Confrontation
text: You gather Fenn, Rhea, and Torque in an empty storage room. None of them know why they're there. Hammer stands behind you with his arms crossed, radiating menace.
You lay out the evidence. The leaked information. The signal piggybacking on your comms. The three of them as the only people with access.

Fenn goes pale and starts talking immediately — defending himself, offering to prove his innocence. Rhea is quiet, watchful, her jaw set. Torque meets your eyes with a steadiness that is either innocence or iron self-control.
'One of you is a traitor,' you say. 'I'd rather give you the chance to confess than have Hammer extract the truth. It won't be pleasant either way, but one path has dignity.'
Silence. Then Torque sighs. 'It's me,' she says. 'And you're making a mistake by giving me time to talk instead of time to run.'

choices.text: "Why? Why betray the Collective?"

ic_064.title: Dran's Judgment
text: Dran listens to your report without interruption. When you finish, he's silent for a long time. His mechanical hand glows hotter — the heat radiating visibly.

'Torque,' he says, the name carrying the weight of years of trust. 'She was one of the first. She helped build this place. She stood beside Hammer when we had nothing.' He closes his eyes. 'Bring her to me.'
Torque is brought to the command table. She doesn't resist. She stands before Dran with her chin raised, defiant even in exposure.
'I need to hear it from you,' Dran says. 'Why?'

'Because they have my daughter,' Torque says. Her voice cracks for the first time. 'Meridian took her six months ago. Told me she'd die in a detention facility if I didn't cooperate. I've been trying to find a way to get her back without —' She stops. 'There was no way out. There's never a way out.'

choices.text: "We rescue her daughter. That's how we handle this."
"She still betrayed us. The damage is done. She has to pay for it."
"Let Dran decide. This is above my authority."

ic_065.title: Torque's Confession
text: Torque's composure finally cracks. She tells you about her daughter, Emi. Fourteen years old, arrested six months ago on fabricated charges. Held in a Meridian black site. Torque was given a choice: cooperate, or Emi disappears permanently.

'I tried to feed them garbage,' she says. 'Useless intel, outdated data. But they have someone who verifies. When the information was bad, they sent me Emi's screams over the comm channel. So I gave them real intelligence. And people got hurt because of me.'
She wipes her eyes with the back of her hand. 'Hate me if you want. I'd do it again. She's my daughter.'
The room is heavy with a silence that has no easy answers.

choices.text: "We'll get your daughter back. The Collective doesn't abandon its people."
"I understand why you did it. But you should have come to us."
"Sympathy doesn't undo the damage. People were arrested because of you."

ic_066.title: Telling Hammer
text: You find Hammer in the training area, teaching a group of new recruits basic self-defense. Watching him move — precise, controlled, patient — you realize that beneath the intimidation is a teacher who genuinely wants his people to survive.
You tell him about Torque. You watch it hit him like a physical blow. His fists clench. His jaw works. For a terrible moment, you think he's going to put his fist through the wall.
'How long?' he asks, his voice barely a whisper.
'Six months. They have her daughter.'

Hammer closes his eyes. When he opens them, something fundamental has changed in his expression. The anger is still there, but it's focused now, precise, aimed at the people who created this situation rather than the woman who was trapped in it.
'We get the girl back,' he says. 'Then we burn the black site to the ground. Then Torque and I have a conversation. In that order.'

choices.text: "Agreed. Let's plan the rescue."

ic_067.title: The Council Vote
text: The Industrial Workers' Protection Act is scheduled for a vote in three weeks. Of the eleven council members, you need six votes. Currently, three are confirmed supporters, three are confirmed opponents (all with corporate funding), and five are undecided.

Spark's analysis identifies the key swing votes: Councilor Marsh, a former factory worker turned politician; Councilor Devi, who represents the commercial district; and Councilor Tan, whose voting record is unpredictable.

'Marsh should be sympathetic, but she's under pressure from corporate lobbyists,' Spark explains. 'Devi is a pragmatist — she'll vote for whatever benefits her district's economy. Tan is a wildcard. Nobody knows what he actually believes.'
You have limited time and resources. Which vote do you pursue first?

choices.text: Approach Councilor Marsh — appeal to her factory roots
Meet with Councilor Devi — frame the Act as economically beneficial
Investigate Councilor Tan — figure out what makes him tick

ic_068.title: The Black Site Rescue
text: Spark locates the black site in a sub-basement beneath a Meridian logistics center. It's off every official record — power siphoned from the city grid, ventilation through converted sewer lines. A place designed to not exist.
You lead a team of eight. Hammer is your second. Torque provides the security codes she was given, buying your team a thirty-second window before the alarms trigger.
The facility is small and brutal. Three holding cells. Interrogation equipment that you don't look at closely. And in the last cell, a girl with her mother's eyes and bruises on her arms.
'Mama?' Emi whispers when she sees Torque.

choices.text: Grab Emi and fight your way out
Use Spark's electronic countermeasures to escape undetected

ic_069.title: Punishment
text: The Collective's justice is swift and harsh. Torque is stripped of her rank, her access, and her weapons. She's given twenty-four hours to leave the Underhammer and told that if she's seen near any Collective operation again, Hammer's people will treat her as a hostile.

Torque takes it without protest. She packs a small bag, pauses at the exit, and looks back at you. 'My daughter is still in that facility,' she says. 'When she dies there, remember that you had the power to help and chose not to.'
She walks out into the Foundry District's perpetual twilight. Hammer watches her go. He says nothing. His silence is worse than anything he could have said.
The mole problem is solved. The cost of solving it sits in your chest like a shard of metal.

choices.text: Focus on the council vote and try to move forward
Secretly mount a rescue for Torque's daughter anyway

ic_070.title: Dran's Decision
text: Dran is silent for a long time. When he speaks, his voice is heavy with the weight of decisions that have no clean answers.

'We rescue the girl,' he says. 'Then Torque stands before the full assembly and answers for what she's done. The Collective decides her fate, not me, not you, not Hammer. We are not a dictatorship. We are not the thing we fight against.'
He places his mechanical hand on Torque's shoulder. The heat must be almost unbearable, but she doesn't flinch.

'I understand why you did it,' he says. 'I might have done the same. But understanding is not the same as forgiveness, and forgiveness is not the same as trust. We'll get your daughter back. What happens after that is up to the people you betrayed.'

choices.text: Plan the rescue mission for Torque's daughter

ic_071.title: Councilor Marsh
text: Councilor Marsh meets you in her office, a modest space decorated with photographs of her factory days. She's in her sixties, grey-haired, with hands that still bear the calluses of a lifetime's labor.

'I know why you're here,' she says. 'And I want to vote yes. I do. But Meridian has made it clear that if I support the Act, they'll fund a challenger in the next election. A young, photogenic corporate loyalist who'll undo everything I've built in eight years on the council.'

She leans forward. 'Give me something I can use. Evidence that supporting the Act is politically safe. Make it so that voting against the workers is more dangerous than voting with them, and you'll have my vote.'

choices.text: Promise to organize a public rally in her support
Offer to provide evidence of Meridian's corruption

ic_072.title: Councilor Devi
text: Councilor Devi is pure pragmatism wrapped in a designer suit. She listens to your pitch about workers' rights with polite attention, then cuts to the chase.

'What does supporting this Act do for my district's economy? The commercial sector depends on factory output. If worker protections increase labor costs, my constituents pay higher prices. Give me an economic argument, not a moral one.'

Spark prepared you for this. You present data showing that improved working conditions increase productivity, reduce turnover, and lower medical costs. Healthier workers produce more. Companies that invest in safety outperform those that don't.
Devi studies the numbers. 'Interesting,' she says. 'Leave the data with me. I'll have my analysts review it. No promises.'

choices.text: Thank her and move on to Councilor Tan
Push harder for a commitment

ic_073.title: Councilor Tan
text: Councilor Tan is the hardest to read. A former academic who entered politics 'on a whim,' according to his bio. He meets you in a tea house in the neutral district, surrounded by old books and the smell of jasmine.

'I've been watching the Iron Collective with great interest,' he says, pouring tea with ceremonial precision. 'You present an interesting case study in revolutionary dynamics. The question I find most compelling is whether your movement can survive success. Revolutions that win often become the new oppressors.'

He sips his tea. 'I'll vote for the Act on one condition: convince me that the Iron Collective will not become another Meridian. That you won't trade one tyrant for another. That power, once gained, will be shared rather than hoarded.'
It's not a simple request. It requires more than rhetoric.

choices.text: "The Collective is democratic. Every decision is voted on by the members."
"Come see for yourself. Visit the Underhammer and meet our people."

ic_074.title: Emi Rescued
text: You get Emi out. The extraction is clean — Spark's countermeasures blind the facility's sensors long enough for your team to move through the corridors like ghosts. Torque carries her daughter in arms that are shaking with something far deeper than adrenaline.

Outside, in the sewer tunnels that lead back to safe territory, Emi clings to her mother and doesn't speak. The bruises and the hollow look in her eyes tell a story that you don't need words to understand.
Hammer stands apart, watching Torque hold her child. His expression cycles through emotions faster than you can track them. Finally, he walks over and places one massive hand on Torque's shoulder.
'We'll talk later,' he says quietly. 'Right now, take your girl home.'
The rescue has cost the Collective resources and attention. But some things matter more than strategy.

choices.text: Return to the Underhammer and refocus on the council vote

ic_075.title: Costly Rescue
text: The extraction goes wrong. Alarms blare. Security drones converge. Hammer holds a corridor alone for two minutes while you and Torque get Emi to the exit — two minutes that feel like two hours and cost him a cracked rib and a concussion.

You escape through the sewer system, carrying Emi between you. She's fourteen and she weighs nothing, hollowed out by weeks of detention. Torque holds her and weeps with the kind of ferocity that could break walls.
Back at the Underhammer, the medic patches everyone up. Hammer waves off his injuries. 'I've had worse on the factory floor,' he says. But he doesn't look at Torque.
The mission is a success, technically. The cost was high. But the Collective has its people back, and that means something.

choices.text: Recover and focus on the council vote

ic_076.title: Building the Coalition
text: Over the next two weeks, the pieces fall into place. Councilor Marsh pledges her vote after a massive rally in the Foundry District draws ten thousand workers. Councilor Devi is swayed by the economic data. And Councilor Tan, after deep deliberation, announces his support publicly.
With three confirmed swing votes added to the three existing supporters, you have six of eleven council votes. Exactly enough.

Dran pulls you aside the night before the vote. 'Tomorrow changes everything,' he says. 'But I've learned not to celebrate until the gavel falls. Our enemies know what's at stake. They'll make a move.'
He promotes you to Commander. 'Whatever happens tomorrow, you've earned this. The Iron Collective doesn't forget its debts. And it doesn't forget its heroes.'

choices.text: Prepare for the council vote

ic_077.title: Tan's Skepticism
text: Councilor Tan shakes his head. 'Democracy is a word people use to describe whatever system they prefer. Meridian calls itself democratic — shareholders vote. The city council calls itself democratic. Neither genuinely shares power with the people most affected by their decisions.'
He finishes his tea. 'I need to see proof, not promises. Come back when you have something concrete to show me.' The meeting is over.
You've failed to convince Tan, but his invitation to prove yourself stands.

choices.text: Invite Tan to visit the Underhammer
Focus on other council votes instead

ic_078.title: Tan's Visit
text: Councilor Tan arrives at the Underhammer blindfolded, per security protocol. When the blindfold comes off, he stands in the main hall and looks around with the measured attention of someone who makes his living observing.

He watches a cell meeting where workers vote on supply allocation. He sits in on a training session where Hammer teaches conflict de-escalation (not just combat, you note with surprise). He talks to workers who tell him their stories — not the polished versions, but the raw, ugly truth of what life in the factories has cost them.

When he leaves, he shakes your hand. 'You haven't convinced me that the Collective won't become corrupt,' he says. 'No one could. Power always corrupts. But you've shown me that these people have built something worth protecting. I'll vote for the Act.'
He pauses at the door. 'Don't make me regret it.'

choices.text: Finalize preparations for the council vote

ic_079.title: The Night Before
text: The Underhammer buzzes with nervous energy the night before the vote. Workers who've never been to city hall iron borrowed clothes. Spark runs final security sweeps. Hammer posts guards at every access point.

You find a quiet moment in the workshop, alone with the sound of cooling machinery. Tomorrow, either the workers of this city gain legal protection for the first time in decades, or the Iron Collective is declared a criminal organization and everyone in this room becomes a fugitive.

Spark finds you there. 'I intercepted a corporate communication,' she says, her face tight. 'Meridian is planning something for tomorrow. I don't know what. The message was heavily encrypted, and I only got fragments. The words I could decode were: contingency, council chamber, and neutralize.'
'They're going to try to stop the vote,' she says. 'We need to be ready for anything.'

choices.text: Increase security around the council chambers
Try to decode the full message before morning

ic_080.title: Council Day
text: Morning breaks over the city like a held breath. Thousands of workers line the streets leading to the council chambers, held back by security barriers but refusing to be silenced. Their chanting vibrates through the building's walls: THE IRON HOLDS.

Inside, the council chamber is packed. Reporters, corporate lobbyists, workers' representatives, and citizens who've come to witness history. The eleven council members take their seats. The Industrial Workers' Protection Act is the first item on the agenda.

You're seated in the gallery with Spark and Hammer. Dran chose not to attend — too recognizable, too much risk. But his voice is in your earpiece: 'Whatever happens, I'm proud of what we've built. All of you.'
The debate begins. The first three speakers argue for the Act. Then a corporate lobbyist takes the floor and begins dismantling the proposal point by point. Then Councilor Marsh stands to speak.

choices.text: Watch and wait for the vote
Scan the chamber for signs of Meridian's contingency plan

ic_081.title: Decoded Message
text: You and Spark work through the night, cracking Meridian's encryption layer by layer. By 0400, you have the full message.

It's worse than you imagined. Meridian isn't planning to disrupt the vote with lobbying or legal challenges. They've hired Ghost Syndicate operatives to assassinate Councilor Marsh during the session. The plan is to blame the killing on the Iron Collective — turning public sympathy against the workers' movement in a single act of manufactured violence.
'We have to warn Marsh,' Spark says, already reaching for her comm unit. 'And we need to identify the operative before they reach the chamber.'
You have three hours before the session begins.

choices.text: Go to the council chambers early and find the assassin

ic_082.title: The Vote Begins
text: The debate runs for three hours. Speeches are made, data is cited, moral arguments clash with economic ones. Through it all, the chanting outside never stops.

Finally, the council president calls for the vote. Your heart pounds. This is it. The first council member votes yes. Then no. Then yes. The count climbs: three for, two against. Four for, three against. Five for, four against.
Councilor Marsh stands. The chamber holds its breath.
A flash of movement in the gallery catches your eye. Someone is reaching into their coat.

choices.text: Shout a warning
Tackle the person in the gallery

ic_083.title: Spotting the Threat
text: Your eyes scan the chamber methodically. Press gallery, public seating, council staff, security personnel. Everyone looks like they belong. That's what makes professionals dangerous.

Then you see it — a maintenance worker near the ventilation access in the upper gallery. The uniform is perfect. The credentials look right. But the worker's shoes are wrong. Tactical boots, not maintenance shoes. And the tool belt has a suspicious weight distribution that suggests something heavier than wrenches.
You signal Hammer with a predetermined hand gesture. He moves immediately, circling toward the upper gallery while you approach from below.
The assassin notices the movement. Professional calm flickers into professional calculation. They reach for their belt.

choices.text: Rush the assassin before they can draw a weapon
Alert council security and let them handle it

ic_084.title: Warning Given
text: Your shout splits the chamber. 'GUN! UPPER GALLERY!' Council security reacts on training, throwing themselves over the council members. The chamber erupts in chaos — screaming, overturned chairs, people diving for cover.
The assassin fires. The shot goes wide, punching a hole in the wall three feet from Councilor Marsh's head. Security returns fire. The assassin is hit, drops, and lies still.

In the aftermath, as medics swarm and security locks the building down, the evidence trail leads directly to Meridian. The assassin's weapon is Ghost Syndicate manufacture. The credentials are Meridian-issued. And the maintenance uniform bears a Meridian subsidiary's logo.
The assassination attempt becomes the story. The vote is postponed, but public outrage ensures it will pass unanimously when it resumes.

choices.text: Wait for the rescheduled vote

ic_085.title: The Tackle
text: You move on instinct. Across the gallery in three strides. The assassin's hand is coming up with a compact pistol when you hit them at full speed, driving them into the railing. The weapon clatters away. Hammer arrives a second later, pinning the assassin with force that ends all resistance immediately.

The chamber erupts in chaos, but the chaos is the manageable kind — shock and relief rather than grief and fury. Councilor Marsh is alive. The weapon, the credentials, the tactical boots — all evidence that leads back to Meridian.
You're bleeding from a cut where the assassin's elbow caught your face. Hammer looks at you with something that might, in another man, be called admiration.
'Not bad,' he says. 'For an organizer.'

choices.text: Demand the vote continue immediately

ic_086.title: The Shot
text: You lunge for the assassin but you're a half-second too slow. The pistol fires. The shot hits Councilor Marsh in the shoulder, spinning her around. She goes down hard.
Hammer reaches the assassin from the other side and ends the threat with a single, devastating blow. Security swarms the gallery. The chamber is screaming.
Marsh survives — the wound is serious but not fatal. You sit beside her gurney as medics work, and she grips your hand with surprising strength.
'Tell them to vote,' she says through gritted teeth. 'Don't let them delay. Vote now, while the blood is fresh and the cameras are rolling. Vote now.'
The evidence connecting the assassination attempt to Meridian is overwhelming. Public fury ensures the Act will pass.

choices.text: Push for an immediate vote

ic_087.title: The Vote Passes
text: The Industrial Workers' Protection Act passes. Nine votes for, two against. The two dissenters are immediately subjected to public scrutiny that reveals deep corporate funding ties. Their political careers end in the same session.

Outside the council chambers, ten thousand workers erupt. The sound reaches the Underhammer through three levels of earth and steel. In the command center, Forge Master Dran sits down slowly and covers his face with his hands. Spark realizes he's crying. She's never seen him cry.

The Act establishes the legal right to organize. It mandates safety standards. It creates an independent workers' advocacy office. It is, by any measure, a revolution achieved through law rather than blood.
But revolutions, as Councilor Tan warned, are defined not by their victories but by what follows.

choices.text: Celebrate, then prepare for the aftermath

ic_088.title: The Aftermath
text: The weeks following the Act's passage are electric with possibility and danger. Workers across the city organize openly for the first time. Factory conditions improve as companies scramble to comply with new regulations. The Iron Collective transitions from an underground movement to a recognized labor organization.

But the corporations are not defeated — they're adapting. Meridian quietly relocates operations to districts outside the Act's jurisdiction. The Neon Court launches a media campaign painting the Collective as dangerous extremists. And smaller companies, unable to afford compliance, begin shutting down, displacing workers who blame the Collective for their job losses.

Dran convenes the leadership. 'The Act was a battle,' he says. 'The war continues. We need to decide what the Iron Collective becomes now. A political party? A labor union? A revolutionary movement? We can't be all three.'
He looks around the table. 'I've led this movement for fifteen years. It's time for fresh vision. I'm nominating a successor.' His eyes settle on you.

choices.text: "I'm honored. I'll lead the Collective into its next chapter."
"The Collective should choose its own leader. Put it to a vote."
"I'm not the right person. I'm a fighter, not a leader."

ic_089.title: Accepting Leadership
text: The weight of Dran's trust settles on your shoulders like a mantle. You accept the nomination, and the room watches to see what kind of leader you'll be.

Hammer steps forward. 'I'll follow your lead,' he says, 'as long as you don't forget where we came from.' Spark nods her agreement. 'I'll keep your secrets and guard your blind spots. Same as I did for Dran.'
The question remains: What direction will you take the Collective?

choices.text: Transform the Collective into a legitimate political movement
Keep the Collective as a militant workers' organization
Expand aggressively — bring every worker in the city under the Collective's banner

ic_090.title: Democratic Election
text: You insist on a democratic process. The full membership votes. Candidates emerge: you, Hammer, and a factory worker named Okoye who represents the rank-and-file perspective.

The campaign is passionate but respectful. Hammer argues for a militant approach — the corporations won't stop, so neither should the Collective. Okoye advocates for integration into the city's political system. You present your own vision.
