When the votes are counted, you win by a narrow margin. But the democratic process itself is the real victory — it proves to Councilor Tan, and to the world, that the Collective practices what it preaches.
Dran embraces you with his mechanical arm, the heat almost painful. 'Better than I hoped,' he says. 'Lead well.'

choices.text: Chart the Collective's future course

ic_091.title: Declining Leadership
text: You decline the nomination. Dran's eyebrows rise — it's clearly not the answer he expected. But he nods slowly.

'There's wisdom in knowing your limits,' he says. 'Not everyone who can lead should lead, and not everyone who should lead wants to.' He turns to the rest of the leadership. 'Then we do this the hard way. We hold an election.'
Hammer is elected as the new Forge Master. His first act is to name you as his chief strategist — a position that carries influence without the burden of ultimate responsibility.
'You'll have my ear,' Hammer tells you. 'Use it wisely. I'm not as patient as Dran.'
The Collective's future will be shaped by Hammer's vision. Harder. More militant. But no less committed to the workers' cause.

choices.text: Serve as Hammer's strategist and face the final crisis

ic_092.title: The Political Path
text: You begin transforming the Iron Collective from an underground movement into a political force. Members run for local office. The Collective opens a public headquarters in the Foundry District. Legal advocacy teams replace sabotage cells.

The transition is painful. Some members feel betrayed — they didn't fight in the shadows to become politicians. Hammer is openly skeptical. 'You're trying to beat them at their own game,' he warns. 'They invented the game. They own the board.'

But the results are undeniable. Within three months, Collective-backed candidates hold seats on four district councils. Workers' grievances are heard in open forums instead of whispered in tunnels. The Foundry District begins to change.
Then Meridian makes its final move.

choices.text: Face Meridian's final challenge

ic_093.title: The Militant Path
text: You keep the Collective as a fighting force. The Underhammer expands. New weapons are manufactured. Training intensifies. The message is clear: the Act may protect workers in law, but the Collective protects them in practice.

Corporate violations are met with direct action. When a factory owner ignores safety regulations, Collective teams shut the factory down. When workers are threatened, armed Collective members escort them to and from their shifts.
The corporations escalate in response. Private armies patrol factory districts. Corporate lobbying pushes for anti-union legislation. The city teeters on the edge of open conflict.
Spark pulls you aside. 'We're on a collision course,' she says. 'Meridian is massing forces. They're going to make their move, and it's going to be big.'
She's right. The final confrontation is coming.

choices.text: Prepare for the final showdown

ic_094.title: Expansion
text: The Collective expands aggressively, absorbing smaller workers' groups and extending its reach into every industrial district. Membership swells to thousands. The red gear symbol appears on walls from the Foundry District to the outer sectors.

But rapid growth brings problems. New members don't share the founding values. Discipline erodes. Some cells begin operating independently, launching unauthorized actions that bring heat on the entire organization. The Collective is becoming too big to control.
Worse, intelligence reports suggest that Meridian has begun planting agents within the new recruits. The very expansion that makes the Collective powerful also makes it vulnerable.
A crisis is brewing. And when it arrives, it arrives all at once.

choices.text: Face the crisis head-on

ic_095.title: The Final Crisis
text: It happens on a Tuesday. Meridian Industries, in coordination with the Neon Court and three other major corporations, announces the Foundry District Restructuring Initiative. Every factory in the district will be automated. Every worker will be replaced. Fifty thousand jobs eliminated in a single corporate decree.

It's not just economic — it's a death sentence for the entire district. Without factory wages, the infrastructure collapses. Without infrastructure, people die. Meridian knows this. They're counting on it.
Dran — retired but never truly gone — appears in the Underhammer for the first time in months. He looks older. Tired. But his eyes still burn.
'This is the moment everything was building toward,' he says. 'Every strike, every speech, every sacrifice. This is the wall. We either break through or we break against it.'
The Collective has one chance. A massive coordinated action — the largest the city has ever seen.

choices.text: Launch a city-wide general strike — shut everything down
Storm Meridian's corporate headquarters and seize control
Call for emergency legislation and a public referendum

ic_096.title: Ending: Revolutionary Victory
text: The general strike spreads like fire through dry grass. It begins in the Foundry District and within twenty-four hours, it engulfs the entire city. Factories, transport hubs, power stations, data centers — everything stops. The great machine of the megacity falls silent for the first time in its history.

The corporations scramble. Automated systems can't replace fifty thousand workers overnight. Supply chains collapse. The stock market plunges. Emergency meetings are called, but there's no one left to take the minutes because the administrative staff is on strike too.

After seventy-two hours, Meridian capitulates. The Foundry District Restructuring Initiative is withdrawn. A new labor charter is drafted — not by corporate lawyers, but by workers' representatives. The Iron Collective takes its place at the negotiating table, no longer as supplicants but as equals.
You stand with Dran on the roof of the Underhammer, looking out over a district that is, for the first time, truly free. His mechanical hand rests on your shoulder, warm as a promise.
'This isn't the end,' he says. 'The fight never ends. But today, the Iron holds.'
He's right. Tomorrow will bring new challenges, new enemies, new compromises. But today, the workers won. And the city will never be the same.

choices.text: The Iron Holds. (Return to main story)

ic_097.title: Storming the Tower
text: Hammer leads the assault on Meridian Tower. Three hundred Collective fighters, armed with salvaged weapons and homemade EMP charges, storm the corporate headquarters in a coordinated attack that overwhelms the private security forces within forty minutes.

You reach the executive floor. The board of directors sits around their polished table, frozen in a tableau of disbelief. These people who decided the fates of thousands from climate-controlled offices have never seen the consequences of their decisions walk through the door.

But victory by force comes with a cost. The city's security forces are mobilizing. The media is broadcasting footage of armed workers smashing through corporate lobbies. Public sympathy is divided — some see heroes, others see terrorists.
Hammer turns to you. 'We hold the tower. Now what? We can't hold it forever.'

choices.text: Use the tower as leverage to force negotiations
Seize Meridian's assets and redistribute them to the workers
Broadcast the executives' own records — let the city see the truth

ic_098.title: Ending: Peaceful Reform
text: You take the fight to the ballot box. Emergency legislation is drafted. A city-wide referendum on the Foundry District Restructuring Initiative is demanded. The campaign is brutal — corporate money floods the opposition, smear campaigns target Collective leadership, and threats are made against workers who support the referendum.
But the people vote. In the largest turnout in the city's history, seventy-three percent vote to block the restructuring. The mandate is undeniable.

The aftermath is slower than a revolution but more enduring. New regulations are written into law. Worker representation is mandated on corporate boards. The Foundry District receives infrastructure investment for the first time in decades.
Dran steps down permanently, satisfied that the Collective has achieved what he always wanted — not the destruction of the system, but its transformation from within.

At his retirement ceremony in the Underhammer, he gives you his mechanical hand's heat core — the glowing heart of the prosthetic that defined him for decades. 'Keep it warm,' he says. 'The forge never cools.'

The Iron Collective becomes the city's first recognized labor party. It's not perfect. It's not utopia. But for the first time, the workers have a voice that can't be silenced, and a vote that can't be ignored.
The iron holds. It holds through law, through patience, through the slow, difficult work of building something better.

choices.text: The Iron Holds. (Return to main story)

ic_099.title: Ending: The Collective Destroyed
text: The seizure of Meridian's assets triggers a full military response. City security forces, supplemented by corporate private armies, lay siege to the Foundry District. The Collective fights with everything it has, but the weight of resources is overwhelming.

The Underhammer falls on the third day. Dran is captured. Spark destroys her equipment and disappears into the city's underground. Hammer fights to the last, covering the evacuation of civilian workers, and is last seen standing alone in a corridor, buying time for people who will never know his name.

You escape through the old sewer tunnels that Spark mapped years ago. Behind you, the Foundry District burns. The Iron Collective is broken — its leadership scattered, its members hunted, its infrastructure destroyed.

But in the refugee camps outside the city, you find workers who still wear the red gear symbol hidden beneath their clothes. In whispered conversations, you hear the words passed between strangers: The Iron Holds.
The Collective is destroyed as an organization. But as an idea, it's harder to kill. Seeds scattered by violence sometimes grow in unexpected soil.
You carry the names of the fallen, the plans for what could have been, and the unshakeable conviction that this fight isn't over. It's just entering a new phase.
One day, the forge will be relit.

choices.text: Carry the flame. The Iron Holds. (Return to main story)

ic_100.title: Ending: Exposed as Double Agent
text: The truth comes out on the eve of the final action. Spark, ever vigilant, discovers encrypted transmissions leaving your personal datapad — transmissions routed to a corporate handler. Every move you've made, every piece of intelligence you've gathered, every person you've recruited — all of it fed to the very corporations the Collective was fighting.

Maybe you were coerced. Maybe you were planted from the start. Maybe the reasons seemed good enough at the time. The details don't matter to the people staring at you now with the specific expression of those who trusted completely and were completely betrayed.
Hammer doesn't speak. He doesn't need to. The look in his eyes says everything — the restraint it's taking him not to kill you is a monument to Dran's teachings about justice over vengeance.

Dran is the last to address you. His voice is quiet, almost gentle, which makes it worse. 'I looked at you and I saw hope,' he says. 'I suppose that was the point. You were designed to be exactly what we needed. What I needed.'

You're escorted out of the Underhammer at gunpoint. Behind you, the Collective scrambles to change codes, relocate cells, and salvage what your betrayal hasn't destroyed. Some of it will survive. Some won't.
You walk through the Foundry District alone, carrying the weight of what you've done. The red gear symbols on the walls no longer feel like invitations. They feel like accusations.
Somewhere in the city, a corporate handler is preparing your payment. Somewhere else, workers are dying because of what you told their enemies.
Was it worth it? The question follows you like a shadow. It will follow you forever.

choices.text: Live with the consequences. (Return to main story)
